id: #Revamped Yimie Quest - Tilted Dagger Part One
trigger: #LET'S GET THIS SHIT DONE ONCE AND FOR ALL

script: AddBarEvent UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_BarEvent "Attempt to communicate with the nervous pilot" "A disheveled pilot sits in the corner of the bar, her eyes darting around frantically as if looking for pursuers." highlight

text:
As you approach the pilot, before you can even open your mouth, she grabs you and pulls you into the booth, and hugs you tightly. You are surprised at first, when you hear some Military Police officers walking past, questioning other patrons about a blonde woman. As they approach, you instinctively put your arms around her.

You take advantage of a passing group of off-duty crew, using them to mask your escape. A few enthusiastic ones jovially bump into you, and they engage you and your companion in spontaneous conversation. In a matter of minutes you blend in with the throng travelling with them to destinations unknown. The woman grips your hand tightly even after you leave the MPs and the crowd far behind.

As you approach the pilot, before you can even open your mouth, she grabs you and pulls you into the booth, and hugs you tightly. You are surprised at first, when you hear some Military Police officers walking past, questioning other patrons about a blonde woman. As they approach, you instinctively put your arms around her.

Appearing like a couple going through a tearful reunion, you both quickly leave the bar for somewhere with more privacy. Using your peripheral vision, you steal a glance. The MPs shadow you from a distance. One of them, seeming to recognize your face says something softly into his radio. An eternity seems to pass. Slowly, you hear the harsh sounds of their boots fade away. Only then does Yimie give a sigh of relief. Neither of you look back until you feel you've left the MPs far, far behind.

After taking some twisting paths and going down a few levels, you let go. A deep sigh escapes her mouth. "Thanks," she mutters breathlessly. After taking a few minutes to calm down, the two of you find another private space to sit down and chat. The more anonymity, the better off you'll be. 

"Yimie Thompson, commanding officer of the 5th Experimental Flight Wing on the Resolution. Or, at least I was." She says hurryingly. Her eyes shift rapidly between you and the entrance. "We were ambushed near Entiaris and they managed to disable the ship. No long-distance comms, so I had to fly here on my own to get help. And when I got here, well…" Her eyes impulsively scan the bar area. Though the two of you are now on a different floor, Yimie is still on high alert.

After giving your name, you ask about her situation. "We had our suspicions when we left port." She says rapidly as she crosses her arms. "Things on inventory were mismatched. Parts and tools going missing. Radars picking up false positives. Marks of sabotage. It was obvious, but Command wanted flight data for a new fighter craft, and we were already under a lot of stress from all these problems stacking up." She shakes her head. 

"I can’t believe she would do something like this." She looks you dead in the eye, her expression darkening. "I think the saboteur was Captain Mizuki, my direct superior and the Resolution’s Aviation Officer. Captain Mizuki was always very secretive. She would occasionally disappear for hours on end only to show up at the end of the day for inspection. On those occasions, something always went very wrong, and it always ended up grounding our test flights the following day."

Yimie's fingers dig into her arms as she clenches. "She left before me to get some help. But even after a few days, no ships came to our rescue. Some of us assumed that she had been killed in transit, but when we checked her quarters, it had been ransacked! Her holopad had been wiped clean, save for a few access records. But checking the ship's data logs, it had been the last thing to access our flight data for the experimental aircraft." She scowls. "I didn't want to believe that she had been a spy. But when I got here, the authorities were already looking for me! They said a survivor from the Resolution had come here, and they pinned me as the traitor!"

Yimie slams her fist into the table. From somewhere above you, you can hear urgent voices. You had a feeling that the MPs would come back. Yimie seemed to sense this too, and grabs your arm. "Please! You have to help me prove that it wasn't me! I know it’s been days, but there has to be somebody still alive on the Resolution! They can vouch for me." The pounding of boots is getting louder. You only have a split second to decide.
options:
UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_YimieOptionA:Promise to find someone or something to prove her innocence
UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_YimieOptionB:Say you can look into it
UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_YimieOptionC:Pull away, saying that this is none of your business
notes: Three options presented. The last one will end the mission abruptly and there's no take back.

script:
AdjustRepPerson uaf_yimie COOPERATIVE 3
#$player.kantaProtection = true
$global.UAFTiltedDaggerPart1RouteAEnd = true
$global.UAFTiltedDaggerPart1ProceedToWreckage = true
UAFTiltedDaggerSpawnZepporiaDerelict
UAFTiltedDaggerSpawnFleet spawnEntiarisPirateFleet
BeginMission UAFTiltedDaggerMission
text:
You take Yimie’s arm and pull her close. Draping your coat over her shoulders, you swiftly lead her out the back door of the bar. Moments later, the MPs arrive at the booth where the two of you had been. Outside, you tell her that the two of you can search for proof of her innocence together, but she pulls away, shaking her head. "The flight data has been stolen, and no doubt someone is looking to sell it. As much as I want to, someone has to stay here and keep an ear to the ground for any suspicious sales of classified data, and between the two of us, only I know what to look out for." Your Tri-pad pings as some coordinates show up on local star charts, out near Entiaris. "You’ll find the Resolution there," Yimie says as the two of you part ways. Hopefully, you’ll be able to find something, a video log, a survivor, anything to help.

You say resolutely, "I promise to help you get out of this."

You react quickly, tying her hair and taking her glasses. The two of you head for a side exit out of the bar, using others to block the MPs’ view. Far enough away, you offer to look into the matter together. Bitter determination fills her voice, "Someone has to stay behind to keep an eye out for the stolen data. Between the two of us, I’m the better fit looking for it." She puts her hand out...
You take her hand firmly and she gives you a pained yet earnest smile.
She puts her hand back down and you see a look of disappointment in her eyes.

text: As your fleet move closer to the wreckage, your bridge comms warned you that there might be trap surrounding the wreckage. With that in mind you decided to proceed.
Having the ambush pirate fleet eliminated, your fleet closes its distance to the wreckage once more.
With no more distractions and possible danger, your own salvagers readies themselves.

You are about to prepare a shuttle towards the wreckage.

You assembled the boarding team who will go with you. After several equipment checks, you board the shuttle and carefully fly towards the UAFS Resolution.

You are about to prepare a shuttle towards the wreckage, however, your communications officer detected multiple signatures from the nearby debris field. It appears that the pirates set up sensor trap and one of your ships tripped it.

Before you can decide your next action, the pirate fleet darts in your direction.
You assembled the boarding team who will go with you. After several equipment checks, you board the shuttle and carefully fly towards the UAFS Resolution.

As you and your teams thoroughly comb the ship, they report back several objects of note. First of all, several of the reported prototype aircraft are missing. Pirates would have likely stripped all of them at once, so it's likely they were used to escape the ship. The next was the locked room. This was, undoubtedly, the ship’s armory. However, there were signs that it had been opened very recently. Pirates are not known for tidiness, and would have left the hatch open after raiding it. Which can only mean that someone was inside.

Your security team rallies behind you as your salvagers get to work cutting the lock. As soon as the door comes off, several gunshots ring out, ricocheting about the room. Through the ringing of your ears, you hear a voice.

"Nobody move!" a panicked woman yells out. "Anyone comes any closer and I’ll space everyone here!" You can make out the faint noise of a pin being pulled. She hasn’t let go of the fuse yet, but your crew is confident they can seal the compartment before the grenade can blow.

text:
"Hah, alright… sure. What else do I gotta lose?" Your salvagers prep the door just in case as one of your security team approaches the hatch, gun raised. Right as he crosses the threshold, two gunshots ring out and he drops dead, followed by a grenade sailing out of the hatch. Expecting this, your security detail swiftly hurls the explosive back inside the armory, and your salvagers are barely able to get the door back in place before an explosion rocks the ship.

The vacuum of space seals the armory door in place. Disappointed, you and your teams return to their search, but turn up nothing. A ping on your TriPad shows a system news headline reporting a pilot has been arrested for treason. With no evidence to the contrary or effective eyewitnesses, there is nothing you can do for Yimie.

Violence has left and only silence remains. Turns out, the crew who died earlier has several friends in your boarding team. They are trying to hold back their anguish.

You return to your fleet afterwards.

Your security crew finally comes in behind you, taking the grenade and reinserting the safety pin. "Ready to talk now?" You ask evenly.

After giving you a piercing look, she wordlessly nods. You get off of her, allowing her to sit up.

text:
"I am Captain Mizuki Yamazaki, a former Aviation Officer aboard the UAFS Resolution." She looks at you dead in the eye. "You're the one in charge here, right? What do you mean that the authorities are looking for Yimie?" 

You fill in about what transpired in Lunamun. Mizuki curses under her breath. "And she thinks I'm the saboteur, huh?" She shakes her head. "Hate to tell you this but it’s not me. As for my disappearances, it’s because I was investigating them myself. I almost had them, when the ambush occurred. While everyone was in a panic, they got the jump on me. When I woke up, I was already locked in here."

SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer1 false

text: "Average family. Unremarkable childhood. Stable career path." She scoffs. "Anything else?"

script:

SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer2 false

text: She gives you an aloof gaze, trying to pierce towards your intentions. "She was a subordinate. Capable, arrogant, and a bit impulsive. A damn good fighter pilot."

options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer3:"How was Yimie’s relation with the crew of the Resolution?"

script:
SetTooltip UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer3 "You already asked this question."
SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer3 false

text: "She wasn't exactly disliked but she could often be found by herself during down time. Fighter pilots have always tended to be a little frictional, and Yimie was no exception. But even then, Yimie was a bit more of a lone ranger than the rest. Honestly, I wouldn't be surprised if some of her squad members had a gripe or two."

script:
SetTooltip UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer4 "You already asked this question."
SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer4 false

text: Mizuki sharpens her gaze. "She wasn't always willing to look at the bigger picture."

options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer5:"Why do we need to get Yimie?"

script:
SetTooltip UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer5 "You already asked this question."
SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartOne_AskMizukiAnswer5 false

text: "Because we do not have time to do things by the book. Every second that passes, the further the project data and prototypes get from us. She knows the fighters better than anyone else still alive."
"Mm."

text:
"I know everyone needs time, but right now, it's not on our side."
OR
"Do not test my patience, starfarer. We are running out of time."
OR
"Fine but know that time is a luxury we can't afford right now."

"Yimie is being held in a holding facility near the spaceport. Relatively speaking, it’s rather lightly protected; most of the security is handled by private security firms. Those are easy. A single properly equipped marine can handle one, man-to-man. Our most pressing concern is the holding facility itself."

Pulling up a map of the facility on her holopad, Mizuki marks the patrols and their routes, as well as shift change times." The holding facility itself is heavily fortified. We have two alternatives…"

text:
"Clearly… Why else would you?" She ponders for a moment.

"Still," Her eyes study you for a moment. "You’ve been doing right by us…" She scowls. "I can’t exactly let her go free, but if you think she’s crucial to finding the real culprit…"

Alexandra raises an eyebrow, "Really now?" She folds her arms. "That puts me in a difficult position. If you’re right and we’re putting an innocent woman on trial, that could bite us later down the line…"

Don’t worry about the higher ups, I’ll keep them off you. Just get me results. You better be right on this, though."

text:
"I see... That will be all then, starfarer. Do see yourself out."

After only a few minutes since leaving Alexandra's company, you hear a chime from your chief of security. "Captain, Auroran troops have stormed and seized the Resolution!" What crew you had aboard the Resolution have been swiftly detained and evicted from the ship. Those who resisted are bruised but otherwise fit for service. Alexandra's request was giving you face.
With Yimie now in your custody, you rendezvous with Mizuki aboard your flagship.

After contemplating for a moment, you tell her that such a hassle isn’t really necessary. Before she can raise a question, you have already left the bar. Within an hour, you and your security team arrive at the holding facility.

text: The guards salute you as you stride straight through the front door and to the holding area. It isn’t long before you arrive at Yimie’s cell. After a few short words with the guard, you and your security team are now "escorting Yimie to a more secure location."
With Yimie now in your custody, you rendezvous with Mizuki aboard your flagship

You decide to process Yimie through medical before meeting her with Mizuki, giving you a moment to breathe. As you tap your TriPad to send the message to Mizuki, you hear an electronic chime from behind you. "Already here, captain $playerName." Mizuki gives you a nod.

A few moments pass on the bridge of your flagship when you hear your security chief call your attention.

"Captain, she's done with medical. Do we still put the cuffs back on? Please advise."
Before you can ask Mizuki to tell you more. The electronic door slides open with a whoosh.

Yimie enters the bridge, displeased. "And here I thought you trusted me, $playerName?!" She spots Mizuki beside you and becomes enraged. Your guards restrain her as she struggles, trying to reach Mizuki.

"Your thieving friends cut you loose? Didn't get your big payday!? Didn't enjoy hanging out in their goddamned filthy rust-buckets!? Huh!?" She speaks venomously, kicking and screaming, managing to cause a guard to fall down with her to the floor. Yimie groans in pain. The other guards rush to secure her.

"Stay back!" Yimie yells. With her right hand, she holds a marine’s sidearm. Her other hand, now dislocated, painfully but firmly braces it steady. She glares hatefully at Mizuki. Your security personnel already have their weapons trained on her. Your hand signal to hold fire is the only thing keeping them from killing Yimie outright.
options:
0:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationRestrainedRouteOptionA:"I'm trying to get your wings back, Yimie."
1:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationRestrainedRouteOptionB:"Commander Thompson, she's not the enemy."

text:
A long, tense moment passes. Everything on the bridge seems frozen in time, save for Yimie’s weapon shaking with barely suppressed rage. After what seems like an eternity, she lets out a sigh and relents, surrendering her pistol.

You call for a medic to the bridge to deal with Yimie’s hand as well as for any other scratches endured by your security teams. There is a perceptible drop in tension as everyone present seems to breathe a sigh of relief. With all the obstacles out of the way, it’s on to the next step, finding the real turncoat.

You call for a medic to the bridge to deal with Yimie’s hand as well as for any other scratches endured by your security teams. There is a perceptible drop in tension as everyone present seems to breathe a sigh of relief. With all the obstacles out of the way, it’s on to the next step, finding the real turncoat.

Something within Yimie snaps at that very moment, and she pulls the trigger. With a resounding crack, Mizuki drops to the floor dead. Without skipping a beat she swings her gun at you, "I’ll kill you, too, traitor!" Your security team springs into action, managing to apprehend her before she can shoot.

Despite her size, she puts up quite the fight, your guards end up with a few broken noses and missing teeth.

options:

1:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeAnythingKnowAbout:"Anything I should know about?"

text: She gives you a brief glance before looking straight ahead. "...She's a good pilot. I have no definitive evidence declaring otherwise. I trust the facts."

She ponders for a moment. "Test Flight Six. Before the Resolution, she and another pilot were competing to see who would be lead pilot on the next flight. They raced toward a small asteroid as the goal post, beyond the testing site. Never saw the pirate frigate until it was within weapons’ range." Mizuki’s eyes sharpen.

"They won. Yimie scored the kill and logged excellent combat data." Mizuki pauses. "Her wingman was not as lucky. That flight ruined one good spacecraft and its pilot."
Before you can ask Mizuki to tell you more. The electronic door slides open with a whoosh.

Yimie swaggers in, flanked by two marines and smiling brightly at you. She jogs toward you excitedly before suddenly stopping dead in her tracks.

Colour drains from Yimie’s face as she sees Mizuki standing beside you. Her breathing gets shallow and she takes a step back looking at you and her in disbelief. "You! What are you doing here, traitor!? Your thieving buddies sell you out? Back to beg us to take you back!? HUH!?"

"Commander Thompson, let’s first listen to the facts–"

Her dialogue is cut short by Yimie’s fist. Your security chief orders the marines to separate the two officers. Realizing that she’s about to be restrained, Yimie singles out one of her would-be captors and tackles him to the ground, before giving him a quick elbow jab to the chest.

"Everyone freeze! Or I gun her down!" Yimie yells, managing to secure a sidearm. Its barrel now aimed directly at Mizuki’s face. Yimie’s rage-filled eyes gaze upon a placid face. Mizuki’s hands are raised, doing her best to come off as non-threatening.
options:
0:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeConfrontOptionA:"Commander Thompson, she is not the enemy."
1:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeConfrontOptionB:"I am on Mizuki's side."
2:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeConfrontOptionC:"Drawing a gun is not the sign of guilt-free conscience."
3:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeConfrontOptionD:"Commander Thompson, don't make this worse than it already is."
4:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeConfrontOptionE:Signal to shoot Yimie
5:UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationLeaveYimieBeConfrontOptionF:"Yimie… Please hear us out."

id: #Goddamnit Venri, this is fucking cancer, I hope you step on a LEGO - Mayu

text:
She hesitates, the rage still etched on her face.

"You would go this far to give her the benefit of the doubt?"

You think quickly. "I would go this far to help you."

Yimie gives you a complicated look.
"Then whose fault is it? Mine!?" Her fingers tighten slightly on the trigger. "And I suppose it's just a coincidence that she just survived?" Yimie shakes her head.
Yimie scoffs. "If it means taking this traitor down with me, then it'll all be worth it." Her fingers remain steady on the trigger.

script: #-rep to alexandra if you pick route C earlier, god fuck this is convoluted
text:
Yimie’s eyes lock onto her target. For a moment you see her stiffen at your words. Her face becomes crestfallen before returning to her angry rage.

"Lost faith in me, starfarer?" She smiles mirthlessly. "Can't say I blame you."

"Don't do anything you’ll regret." Mizuki says, hands held high.

"I won’t." She says as the bullet leaves the barrel.

In a handful of seconds, Yimie is gunned down by your troops. Her and Mizuki’s corpses lie bloody on the floor of the bridge. It doesn't take long for her body to be collected. In fifteen minutes, your bridge is already scrubbed clean. The medical team surveys what few scratches the incident marked on your personnel. However, you and the crew carry on. An unfortunate, if not regrettable, end to the affair.

options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationPostConfrontationTalkToYimieCallYimie15Cont:"Can you tell me about Test Flight Six?"

text:
Yimie makes a complex face. "We were just racing. Earlier patrols cleared the test flight area. No idea how they snuck in but there they were." She says reliving the moment.

"Ian was the first to spot it, yelling how it wasn’t supposed to be there. I was right behind him, fingers right on the trigger, ready to follow up as soon as he got out of the way. Those five seconds were way too long. His plane got punctured with holes before he could land a shot. Didn’t find out ‘til later but that brief AA burst from their PDs knocked out his targeting system. Guy couldn’t even fire even if he came back. After he left me and the pirate frigate alone, I just unloaded on ‘em. Don’t really remember any of it. I just remember climbing out of the cockpit and the designers and maintainers slapping me on the back like I’d beat the entire Heg all by my lonesome."
Yimie sighs.
options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwo_YimiePostRescueOperationPostConfrontationTalkToYimieCallYimieContTestFlight6Cont:"There’s more to this story, isn’t there?"

text:
"You catch on quick." Yimie says regretfully.

"Ian was never the same after that. He’d freeze up in the flight-sim every time there was a bogey. Guy just… couldn’t pull the trigger." Yimie looks at you with serious eyes. "Can you imagine a fighter pilot not being able to fight? Not being able to have your back when it counts?" Yimie shakes her head. 

"He lost his wings in that mission. He became a rhean. A flightless bird. It is the greatest insult you can call an Auroran pilot." Yimie clenches her hand in yours.

script:
SetTextHighlightColors "235,105,125,255"
SetTextHighlights "She sits there in silence, hand tightly holding yours, gripping it for dear life for a moment that can never be long enough"
FireAll UAFTiltedDaggerPartTwoYimiePostRescueOperationPostConfrontationTalkYimieOptions
text:
"If I had been faster, I would have been there first. Maybe Ian wouldn’t have lost his trigger sense, wouldn’t have had to lose his wings." She looks at you with eyes full of… anger? No. Frustration. "I swore I’d always be first in the fire, never hesitating in the face of the enemy. Pirates. Heg. Ludd nutcases. Or whatever else. I’d never lose anything more than necessary."

She sits there in silence, hand tightly holding yours, gripping it for dear life for a moment that can never be long enough.

text:
"Busybody, aren’t you?" She says exasperated. "Fine, I’ll bite. My family was military. Hard head, hard ass, hard life. That’s what my father used to say. Maybe they had a point. I was good at my jobs and I never left things half-assed." A smug smile creeps onto her face.

"My family was serving the Queen in some form or another for as long as I can remember, so when she wound up here, no surprise I wound up here alongside her."

text:
"Indeed. He may have gotten the jump on me aboard the Resolution, but I managed to tag him before he left. As our friend here was getting you out of prison, I connected to their comms network and find a trail leading right to him." Mizuki hands you her holopad. The signal’s last transmission appears to be from the Lanun Listening Post. "Had our opponent been anyone else, they might have found the tracker. We’re lucky that the lower ranks of the Luddites are pretty much tech illiterate. Led us right back to home base before they figured out they were being watched."

"We better move fast, though." Mizuki said as she took her holopad back. "I have no doubt those rats are scurrying about, trying to get the hell out of there before the authorities show up."

Mizuki looks at the rest of you. "Any questions?"

"We better move fast, though." Mizuki said as she took her holopad back. "I have no doubt those rats are scurrying about, trying to get the hell out of there before the authorities show up."

Mizuki looks at the rest of you. "Any questions?"

options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionA:"How'd you know the traitor was a Pather?"

script:
SetTooltip UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionA "You already asked this question."
SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionA false

text: "One of the sensor analysts came to me about a strange frequency. At the time, it could have been chalked up to noise, signals from long dead wrecks bouncing out towards our vessels. Unlucky traders getting snatched by pirates or raiders still broadcasting their distress calls. He was just going off his gut feeling so I only told him to look into it on his off-time. When he managed to intercept a portion of the transmission, it was just a vague unassuming query. It was when we detected someone was responding with some of our own on-board data that I knew we had a traitor on board. Would end every transmission with ‘Ludd be praised.’ I had made plans to apprehend him on the day of the attack. You know the rest."

options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionB:"Any chance they could release the data to the open market?"

script:
SetTooltip UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionB "You already asked this question."
SetEnabled UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionB false

text:
"For now? No. Judging by their most recent comms-chatter, they’re not willing to do anyone any favors. The cell responsible would rather keep the prize for itself, which means they’re going to have to run. We have time while they gather all their resources before pulling out, but the window is closing."

"Guess I don't need to monitor the market anymore." Yimie says with an edge in her voice.
The drive back to your ships smoothly matched only by the silence of the two women in the back. Your crew quickly prepares for departure.

options: UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_FavoniusMissionBriefingOptionD:"I have some things to reorganize first."

text: Mizuki furrows her brows. "I understand but we don't have a lot of time to waste. Be quick about your preparations."

As you arrive in orbit, a large amount of Pather ships emerge from the station, forming a significant fleet. They shadow your ships and block your approach. Your fleet then receives a video transmission on an open channel.

"Heathen, your efforts to halt the will of Ludd cannot be ignored. Your assistance to those that would conspire with and harbor those soulless abominations is abhorrent and vile. Truly, Ludd tests us in excruciating ways. We shall keep our faith, foul heathen, and take peace in your demise!"
You've arrived just in time! Hunt them down and make sure they cannot escape.

script:
SetTextHighlights "Your bridge crew informs you that Yimie and Mizuki have already descended with fireteams during the naval engagement"
$global.UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_LanunPatherTraitorKilled = true
Call $global.UAFTiltedDaggerMissionPartThree updateStage
$global.UAFTiltedDaggerPartThree_LanunPostDialog = true
text: With any would-be escapees now spaced or stranded, you turn your attention to the listening post itself. Your bridge crew informs you that Yimie and Mizuki have already descended with fireteams during the naval engagement. You prepare to catch up with them.

You head toward Lanun’s docking area, and after navigating towards the appropriate areas, find Mizuki watching Yimie crawl over a strike craft parked in a newly unsealed fighter bay. "Hey!" Mizuki calls out to you with a bit of pride. "You’re finally here! We got here just in time, too. It looks like they were packing up the Hyper Aria prototype when they learned we were onto them. Yimie’s giving her a once over before we retrieve her to make sure nothing is missing. In the meantime, we better do a sweep before we leave. You may have put the fleet out of action, but there could still-"

Yimie cries out from beside the craft. Turning, you see that she’s scrambling towards you as gunfire rings out from the far side of the hangar. You, Mizuki, and your security team spring into action. For several minutes you trade fire with what seems to be an endless amount of Pathers.

At some point you stopped keeping count of how many you've gunned down, moving from one to the next. Thankfully, you and your team get the upper hand and begin to advance on the enemy. Sensing the shift in the battle, the enemy makes a break for the last remaining frigate on the station. As it takes off, your ships open fire on it. A lucky shot clips the engine, yet it manages to take cover in the nearby asteroid belt. It won't be long before it escapes.

Returning your attention to your surroundings, you find that most of your casualties are minor… Save for Mizuki. From your cursory glance, you see two shots to the chest. She needs surgery.

You look for Yimie, finding her fuming. Before you can call out to her, she immediately jumps into the cockpit of the Hyper Aria in order to pursue the frigate. You reach for your commlink, connecting to her as she begins to run pre-flight checks

text:
"Then you know I can't follow that order."

The jet whines to life before roaring to chase after the fleeing Pathers. You go on your comms urgently seeking another transport for Mizuki. It isn't long before you hear reports of Yimie hosing the frigate with tracer-fire. The medic shakes his head at you. Mizuki does not survive the hour, and you close her eyes just after her vitals cease. Yimie reports success, with some triumph in her voice. You feel little of it while your hands are still slick with Mizuki's blood.

text: After dealing with Mizuki's body, you write your initial report of the situation to the UAF. With plans to send it back once you reach the ideal comms relay, you make your way back to Nur.

Yimie takes off in pursuit, leaving you and your team to escort the fatally wounded Mizuki back to the dock. It takes you a few minutes for you and your teams to get her a transport. As you prepare for liftoff, you see a burst of orange behind the transport.

The frigate erupts into a fireball, spilling debris. Yimie reports success with a hint of pride, but Mizuki dies en route back to the fleet. You feel little glory. With hands slick with Mizuki’s blood, you reach up to close her eyelids. Revenge on her killers is cold comfort.

text: Asking around for Yimie leads you to the hangar. As she talks to your maintainers about what the prototype needs, you see an enthusiasm and peace that suits her quite well. As she goes over the cockpit, no doubt making microadjustments to suit her style.

You call out to her.

"Boss!" She jumps down from the cockpit, landing toward you. "Isn’t she a beauty?" She gestures to the fighter, clearly mesmerized. "She's one of the fastest babies in the fleet, with the fireworks to match." She can't stop gushing about its turning speed, armaments, security features, and more besides. After a few minutes of this, she catches herself. She tucks a strand behind her ear, clearly embarrassed. 

"So what brings you here, $playerName?"

text:
Yimie becomes a shade of scarlet. "Who's your pilot!?" Realizing her overreaction, she coughs. "Captain, I think it would be best if we did not give each other premature ideas of the future." 

She looks around, making sure no one is looking before placing a soft peck on your cheek. A bashful smile is etched on her face. "Wouldn’t want to give anyone false hopes now, would we?"

id: # Opt A - B (Yimie Recruit if picked)

text:
Yimie looks away, tugging at her. "Ahem, well, as you can see I’m fine." Her bashful face is red, her blue eyes unable to meet yours.

"Clearly." You say.

"Just because you saved my ass, doesn’t mean you get the privilege of poking fun at me." She says poking at your chest. By the time you register the kiss on your cheek, it’s already over. "Consider that a thank you. Asshole."

id: # Opt A - C (Great, another fucking branching wow fuck you)

text: "Thanks for checking how I was doing. Honestly… It’s been a lot, but I’m hanging in there." She gives you a resolute smile, before her expression suddenly dampens. "I just didn’t want them to hurt anyone else… Like they did me. Do you think she was worth it?" She looks to you for an answer.

You take a moment. "They’re not worth a single hair on any of us. One Mizuki could help take down a thousand of those Pathers. So can I. So can you." Yimie nods in understanding. "If, and I mean big ‘IF’, I get my wings back, would you mind if I tagged along?"

id: # Opt A - C - A (Yimie Recruit if picked)

id: # Opt B - A (Mizuki Recruit if picked)

text: "Seems so," Curious, you ask half-jokingly, "So what reward do I get?" Running a fleet can’t be done on charity alone. You know this better than anyone.

Mizuki scoffs in response.

"Well, aren’t you the avaricious one?" For a brief moment you think you see her smile. "Surely we can discuss compensation when I get out of this damned bed, no?"

"No can do." You assure her you will not hold saving her life over her.

"Hmph. Well, aren’t you the selfless hero? If that’s your response, then I shall consider the matter closed."

id: # Opt C - Wrap up and fuck off from Lanun

text: Leaving Lanun goes smoothly. With the site fully processed, you order your crew to head back to Nur, while simultaneously preparing to contact them with your initial report.

After making contact with your superiors, they respond with commendations and accolades for you and the two central officers in this incident. Though a remnant of the Pather cell responsible remains at large, you prevented the technical data and the prototypes from falling into their hands.

During what the top brass is calling the Resolution Incident, you helped clear Yimie Thompson's name with Mizuki. Both officers are now undergoing reassignment.

As for rewarding your part in this matter, UAF High Command struggled with considering how to address your choice not to turn in a person-of-interest during an ongoing investigation, as well as for obtaining confidential military secrets. A generous sum of hush money in the form of "material reimbursement" is transferred to you in exchange for your silence.

"As for the Hyper Aria prototypes, we can't exactly put spilled milk back into a broken glass. So as part of the compensation package, you will be allowed to retain the prototypes you secured, though its maintenance will also be solely your responsibility." Said Omiko, Alexandra's liaison officer. "Just remember to send us back the flight data and be discreet about its specifications. This concludes our business, captain."

text:
"Tut, tut, such a mischievous starfarer. Such a clumsy attempt at acquiring a high-end military asset. A ship goes missing, lost in the void of space, feared destroyed, and yet you dare to bring it to my station without a word of explanation?" The administrator's voice is soft and polite as she scolds you. You see her take off her glove and your face is stinging before you can register what happened.

Alexandra let's out an exasperated sigh. "If I may request one thing... be craftier than that. I managed to ease some tensions by seizing the ship and processing it by the book, convincing all relevant parties that you had willingly surrendered it for official investigation. Bless your kindhearted soul. But repeat this kind of carelessness again and I will not save you. Now, if you have learned your lesson, do enjoy your stay. Good day."

I really want to relax a bit but this station needs more approval and overseeing as it is still growing, there is also this merchant that has been screwing things up near the dock, like who bring their Lava-pet around?! it absolutely melts the railing and now we need to have engineer to repair that thing! -"

You blink. It seems to have disappeared from your vicinity.
The creature you've seen scuttling before is back again. A morbid curiosity is welling up inside you. You wonder why there's an animal in such place.

[The chances of you getting killed by Persephone are few, but never zero.]

You wonder what it means but you shrug it off as trivial vandalism. As you turn back, someone stands in your way, preventing you from proceeding.

Standing before you is a sharply dressed woman in military garb. Her lavender hair 

Her words bring a chill to your spine. "Hello, $playerName. How fast can you run?"

"It is theorized that it takes 28 stab wounds to kill someone in record time. Would you like to find out?"

After hearing such words, your brain seems to ignite itself, expanding beyond its limit to deal with the strange woman. Without wasting a single second, your body is already sprinting away before you realize it.
With all of your might and heightened sense of self-preservation, you run away from her without a single glance back.

text:
Curious, you rummage through the pile only to discover that there is a functional blueprint of some sort.

It's unusual to see something important lying alongside the trash. Someone must have overlooked and threw it here. Whatever the reason, the blueprint is now yours.

Curious, you rummage through the pile only to discover that there's a data storage device. The item is still functional but you notice that there is a security lock that prevents any access to its contents.

You contact a tech specialist from your fleet and inform them about the data storage device. They instruct you to connect your TriPad to the device, through which the specialist starts remotely decrypting each layer of security.
A few minutes later, they successfully gain access and extract the protected file. "It's done! We've got it, captain!" You thank the specialist and end the call.

After placing the opened can of seafood paste on the ground, you hide behind stacks of boxes. Silently, you lie in wait for the feline to appear.

Not long after, the seafood paste's strong scent has successfully attracted the kitten. It cautiously scans the area before taking the bait. Now, that the kitten has been occupied, you approach it with extreme care, slowly but surely closing the distance. The kitten's head, as if clairvoyant, suddenly turns to you before jolting away towards the pile of rubbish, using it as a platform from which to better scamper up the wall.

Aware of the slim chance of success, you shrug off your defeat. As you walk away, you squint your eyes and notice something glinting on the ground; it must have fallen down from the pile when the kitten made its escape.
options: UAFMysteriousKittenChaseAreaCShapeTeeTopAreaLeftPathGetItemCPickup:Take a closer look and pick it up

text:
You pick up the object and give it a thorough inspection. It turns out to be a blueprint of a fighter, however, the data seems incomplete. Perhaps studying it more closely later might lead to more clues.

For now, you resume the hunt.

It's a dead end.

Nope, there's only trash here.
You continue trudging this path.
It's a dead end.

It's a dead end. But something isn't right. It's eerily quiet. You are now fully aware that this part of the alley is secluded away from the main foot traffic of the station.

As soon as you turn around, a female voice calls out to you, "H-Hello, $playerName." Someone is standing below the lamp post and is slowly approaching you.
options: UAFMysteriousKittenChaseMiddleAreaPathDeadEndAltCont:"Who are you? Why do you know my name!?"

text:
She continues walking towards you and stops. The strange woman holds a... notebook? Wait, it's more like a dossier.

"According to this file, y-you are Subject Number: 621." At first glance, she seems harmless. Yet, there's an unusual air about her and her sudden appearance.

"I-I understand if you are confused but I've been dying to meet you in person. I'm Sophia!" She then resumes walking toward your direction but something drops from her lab coat.

A metallic sound rings out. You focus on the fallen object, your mind is a little slow to recognize it. Is that... a scalpel? Why would anyone bring a scalpel? A stream of questions runs through your head.

She picks it up, however, she fumbles and thus drops more tools from her pocket. "Oops, my bad. Hehe..." Feigning ignorance about her earlier clumsiness, she continues to fumble. This time it's a Leucotome - a surgical instrument used for performing leucotomies (also known as lobotomies) and other forms of psychosurgery.

Now, you start to feel in danger. Why would anyone carry a tool like that? You begin to devise your escape while she is occupied.

Whoever that Sophia was, she's a hazard to your health no matter how you look at it.

It's a dead end. But suddenly, you feel a presence behind you. You turn around to confirm your suspicion.

What you see is a woman in military uniform adorned with multitudes of medals. One glance and you can tell that she's a high ranking official in the Federation. For some reason, she's scowling at you.

And then the woman speaks. "So, you saw it? I'm sure you did!"

"Don't HUH me! I know what you did!" You have no idea who she is and what she's talking about.

Confused. You try to remember if you wronged her somewhere in the past. You rack your brain to the point that you get a headache. Unfortunately, nothing rings a bell.

To diffuse the situation, you try to answer her.

text:
Finally, after traversing the maze-like alleyway, you spot the elusive creature.

Fortunately, it seems that it's not aware of your presence. Something else has grabbed its attention. This is your chance to catch it.
options:
0:UAFMysteriousKittenChaseEnd:Attempt to catch it
1:UAFMysteriousKittenChaseBeginAreaA2UpwardPathwayIntersectionRight:Go back (Return to previous path)

text:
You carefully approach the kitten, you begin to tip-toe so as not to alert the little creature. After a few more steps, you prepare yourself to grab it.

Until— "!!!" The kitten immediately turns to your direction. Startled, it lets out a cry and darts away from your grasp. You are exhausted. You contemplate what you are doing — you realize how ridiculous it is to be chasing a small kitten in a secluded area unknown to you.

And then you look ahead. You see a massive steel door, wide enough to fit a small space shuttle into. It seems solid and looks recently cleaned, starkly contrasting with the worn surroundings.

As you approach the structure, you notice that you aren't alone. You see a woman looking up at the strange structure. She seems to be lost in her own world.

You call out to her.

She looks in your direction after hearing your voice. She gives you a complicated expression, before smiling at you as usual. Nia starts walking up to your direction. "I did not expect to see you here of all places, captain. Do you happen to be lost?" she asks, pleasantly.
options: UAFMysteriousKittenStrangeBuildingNiaMeetAnswer:Explain that you are chasing a kitten

text:
Nia Aurora smiles, amused at the idea of you ending up here because of a kitten. "My, that's very dedicated of you. You really played with Kaavi."

You give Nia a puzzled look. She bends down to pet the white and grey cat. Kaavi nuzzles against her hand. Nia's smile looks so soft. "Such a mischievous child. He likes to play games with people and you are just the latest victim."
options: UAFMysteriousKittenStrangeBuildingNiaMeetReply:"I guess so... but what is this place?"

text:
Nia looks up at the structure again and answers your question. "Hmm... I suppose you're trustworthy enough. Alright, I'll tell you."

She pauses for a bit and then starts explaining, "It may look like your usual abandoned warehouse, but really, it's a hidden hangar for a strikecraft I made a long time ago."

"Yes, a strikecraft. It would be better if you see it for yourself. Come with me." You follow Nia towards the entrance of the hangar.

She approaches the access panel for the smaller man-sized entrance and proceeds with giving her biometrics. Once the access verification is done, she prompts you to get inside of the facility.

The lights automatically light up one by one as you walk through the corridors. The interior of the hangar is surprisingly more well-maintained than the entrance.

As you step further, you can now see the strikecraft deep in its slumber at the center of the hangar. Nia stops in her tracks and makes a welcoming gesture. "Captain $playerName, welcome to the project site of the Lynx."

"During my days in the Auroran R&D division, my research team came up with the idea for a strikecraft capable of multi-combat." Nia walks closer to the Lynx and gently touches its exterior.

"However… At some point in its development, we reached an impasse. Time was running out for development at the time. Our resources were limited. Between the other more promising strikecraft programs and ours which ran over its budget, it's hardly a surprise that our superiors opted to abandon it."

Nia sighs bitterly. "Indeed. Her weapon systems are complete, but her most crucial part is yet to be finished. The multi-combat subsystem." She looks at you with her usual smile. Her eyes seem to go through a series of emotions, curiosity, wariness, reluctance.

"I'll send you her documentation." she says as if surrendering. Nia taps her TriPad and sends you the specification of the Lynx.

"Her modular weapon systems are already functional except for the subsystems." Nia ponders deeply about something.

"Maybe this is a terrible decision but perhaps… meeting you here is more than just coincidence. Knowing your skills, and your track record for fortune, I think you can help me finish what I have set out to do."
options: UAFMysteriousKittenStrangeBuildingNiaMeetReplyCont6:"Okay then, what can I do to help?"

text:
"I'm so glad that you are willing to help. Well then, what you must do is quite simple. You just need to take her with you. Make her a part of your fleet and deploy her in combat."

"I will install a program that will record all of its real-time combat data, and will automatically transmit the data to me directly."
options: UAFMysteriousKittenStrangeBuildingNiaMeetReplyCont7:"Understood, I can pretty much do that."

text:
"Wonderful! I'll make the arrangements so she can join your fleet. It won't take long. Captain—" Nia steps closer to you and extends her right hand. "You have my gratitude, captain. Because of this, she can take flight in the cosmos once more."

You shake hands with her. Nia's smile reaches her eyes. You enter the Lynx's cockpit while she leans over the side explaining its Automata-hybrid classification. It can be converted to a non-automated ship through a certain method.

"Wonderful! I'll make the arrangements so she can join your fleet. It won't take long. Captain—" Nia steps closer to you and extends her right hand. "You have my gratitude, captain. Because of this, she can take flight in the cosmos once more."

You shake hands with her. Nia's smile reaches her eyes. You enter the Lynx's cockpit while she leans over the side explaining its Automata-hybrid classification. When she moves to the technicalities, you remember the blueprint you picked up. Now might be the time to bring this up to Nia.

Nia giggles in response. "I see. To think you would find this blueprint in the trash heap because of Kaavi. Hmm, alright. It's a blueprint for the Lynx with missing crucial schematics. Even if someone were to pick it up, it would be incomplete and therefore useless."

You hand over the blueprint to Nia, who connects it to the Lynx's control panel. "Since you will be the new owner of this strikecraft, I might as well save you the trouble of reverse-engineering it as a gift. Give me a moment, please." After a few gestures a holoscreen appears. She interacts with it as it hovers above the blueprint, expertly filling in the missing data, completing it.
options: UAFMysteriousKittenStrangeBuildingNiaMeetReplyCont7ALTEndingCont1:"Very generous of you. I'll take good care of it."

text:
"Indeed, you will. The Lynx is important to me so do your best. Additionally, the file transfer will be done soon." It takes her a couple of minutes to finish the process. Nia unplugs the blueprint and then she hands it back to you.

"Do not get any strange ideas of selling this to the pirates or anywhere else. I'll be quite upset if you do." She flashes her most dangerous smile, and you feel a chill crawl up your spine.

text:

A few hours later,  $personFirstName has finished the combat program. She stretches her arms before standing up. "It's done; all that's left is to install the program into a black box."

"It's a computational matrix contained in a sub-processor. This is the reason why I asked you to get me those raw materials. Anyway, I'm uploading it to the black box as we speak."

Once the upload is done, $personFirstName runs multiple system checks before sending it to your ship's cargo hold. "Seems that we are all done here. Give the black box to Chris—I mean Administrator Aurora, and she will handle the rest."

OR

"Send me the Lynx and the black box. I need to calibrate the program first before I integrate it."
options: UAFLynxUpgradeLevelReturnIntroCont:Send the black box and the Lynx

text:
The Lynx was towed into Vermilion Station's service bay.

"I'll complete the last step. Worry not, captain. This won't take long." Nia immediately begins running system checks, calibrating and preparing the black box for integration.

An hour has passed; Nia has successfully integrated the black box into the Lynx. "Captain, it is done. I'll give you the full details of its newest capability." Nia sends you a file.
Detailed information of the Second Wind function appears on your screen.

"Indeed. I'm quite sure it will be useful to you, captain. I would like to thank you again for your contributions to the further development of the Lynx. We are now truly nearing its completion..."

For a brief instant, you think you see Nia gently smile at you. "I'll let you know if I need your help again. I'll see you again, captain." She sees you off with her usual polite smile.

You ask Nia why she looks distressed. 

She turns to you, a grim look on her face. "We have a situation. I received a report that someone snuck inside the fighter research facility at Nova Drive Yards. It appears that someone from our side went rogue, sabotaged the facility, and stole top-secret data and documents. Furthermore—" She pauses, lets out a heavy sigh, before giving you an uncharacteristically cold look.

"Some of the stolen data involves Project Lynx. It holds the last piece necessary for us to complete the Lynx's final system."
options: UAFNiaLynxUpgradeStageFinalIntroCont:"Any leads about the identity of the thief?"

text:
"Yes, in fact, I know who it is," she says decisively, sharply, almost mechanically so. "His name is Theodore Cohen, a former weapons systems researcher of the Federation." She taps her console, sending Cohen's dossier to your TriPad.

"A cunning man with an obsessive personality. We often clashed during our days back at Nova Drive Yards. It all came to a head when I denied funding for his research that had questionable ethics. Things between us were never the same after that. I hoped my transfer would ease the enmity between us. That does not seem to be the case."

"Prior to that, he was heavily involved in Project LYNX; however instead of generating better equations to optimize the Lynx's adaptability, he suggested taking a true living human brain from comatose refugees to be the center of the multi-combat system. He believed our artificial intelligence solution to be too costly, too time-consuming and too resource-heavy. He was shocked at the team's complete rejection of the idea."

"He continued to work diligently, with his usual obsessiveness, silently coding away. Until now, I had always thought his criticisms and rigidity came from a place of honesty and sincerity. Now, I'm no longer sure." By the time she finishes, you wonder where the kind and polite administrator has gone.
options: UAFNiaLynxUpgradeStageFinalIntroCont2:"From the looks of it, you hid the Lynx well."

text:
"Untrue. I just couldn't let it go. I kept working, kept hoping... banking on the slim probability that perhaps one day she would soar." Again, she steels herself.

"We cannot let Cohen leave with our data. I think I can speak for the team that built her when I say that no one would want her falling into malicious hands." She looks straight into your eyes with grim determination.

"That's why captain $playerName, I am asking for your help once more. Please, retrieve the data."
options: UAFNiaLynxUpgradeStageFinalIntroCont3:"Alright, I'll hunt him down. Tell me his location."

text:
Nia's face brightens slightly. "Very good." Nia taps her console again, eager to send you on your way. The starmap of Caeli flashes up on your screen. "His runaway fleet was last spotted in the Caeli star system. We assume that he is going to rendezvous with pirate contacts in Los Scrapyard, orbiting Atropos, an icy, rocky planet that is skirting the outermost fringe of the system." 

"It matters not if he is alive or dead. Or better yet, leave him dead so long as you recover the data from him. Do be advised that not only Project Lynx but several other important research and Federation security documents are at stake here. So, please, stop him at all costs."
options: UAFNiaLynxUpgradeStageFinalIntroCont4:"Got it, I'll be on my way."

text:
"Thank you, captain. Good hunting."

For once Nia does not give you her usual smile but a look of grim encouragement.
options: UAFNiaLynxUpgradeStageFinalIntroEnd:Cut the comm link and leave

text:
"Hmm, what's this? Did someone from the Federation send you to chase me?" He raises his brow. "Oh, I do have an inkling who sent you here, it must be that icy witch, Christine Forstner."

"Ah, I get it now! You must be her lapdog, running around like a faithful slave. Does it please you to be doing menial chores for her? I pity you, mutt."

The man sneers at your response. "Do your worst! Those fools from the Federation are too shortsighted to make the right decisions. I know you have the Lynx, I bet you are taking advantage of its variable weapon systems. You can thank me for that."

"Well, it doesn't matter. I can create a better strikecraft. My ambitions are far too noble to be contained within the confines of the Federation. There are others out there who are willing to match my steps, willing to join me in achieving greatness. Once I get rid of you, I shall rise again in the core worlds!"

text:
"I see. So, you managed to retrieve the stolen data and documents. Excellent work, captain." You send the documents and devices safely secured within a physical storage unit to Nia Aurora under heavy guard. After a few moments, they arrive in her office.

After carefully reviewing and verifying each of the contents, she lets out a sigh of relief. "I'm glad that everything remained intact, including the Project Lynx data. With this, we can finally complete its final systems."
options: UAFLynxUpgradeLevelFinalReturnIntroCont:"Do I need to do anything else?"

text:
"I appreciate your initiative, but no need to worry about any extra work. Theodore seemed like quite the fool, tweaking the framework schematics and inserting his own fresh equations and technical inputs. Meaning—" She taps her console; a holographic model of the Lynx appears on your screen.

"He just helped us complete the Lynx's development." Nia begins typing with haste. "All I need to do is reconfigure and clean up certain parts of the framework, and then install the completed program into the Lynx's operating system. It never occurred to him that he might lose, so he went all-out to improve the framework. Despite his improvements, it was clear he believed that substituting electronic systems for organic ones was optimal. Tri-Tachyon would have surely been interested. As for the rest, who can say? A system with the efficiency of an AI but with the will of a human? Who knows what could have been?"
options: UAFLynxUpgradeLevelFinalReturnIntroCont1:"So, the last upgrade can be done right now?"

text:
"Yes. I'm doing the final checks and removing any possible margin of error as we speak. Give me a moment and I'll have this done." Nia stands up, moving to her other desk; she begins typing rapidly at her console. You can hear her mumbling to herself as she codes.

Not long after, she returns to your screen. "It's done! Allow me to introduce the defining feature of the Lynx and its last capability, the Cosmo Grazer!" The hologram of the Lynx spins and expands, allowing you to zoom in towards its center. Detailed information about the ability flashes on your screen.

"I'm going to install the program remotely into the Lynx, no need to send it to the station's port." The installation takes a couple of minutes and afterwards she tells you that everything is in place.

"And there you have it. I didn't expect that this day would come. I have witnessed its completion, and it's all thanks to you, captain. I am truly grateful for your help…" Nia smiles gently, yet tiredly, at you.

"I'll inform my former colleagues on the project. I'm sure they will be quite elated to know that the Lynx has been completed. Please, take good care of the Lynx, captain. I'll be seeing you."

text:
"Sure thing, since you have proved yourself useful to the station, I'll gladly help you procure the item."
Alexandra whispers to one of her bodyguards, who promptly activated a communication device on their person, relaying contingency codes to their peers.

"Please reclaim it from the General Commissioner. I gave them a heads-up."
Alexandra smiles at you.

"Hmm. But of course, it would have to be the Choco Lava cake from New Aurora. That pastry is a common dessert for our people. I think every Auroran has fond memories partaking in it with loved ones."

She pauses for a beat, pondering.

"It would be nice if I can share them with my staff on the station. I'd need 300 of them, but sadly I can't seem to make the time to get them myself."
options: 1:UAFVNBackOption:"I see. Thank you for indulging my curiosity."

text:
"I don't have a particular snack in mind, but I heard that while Choco lava cake from Auroria is a delicacy, their donuts are far more popular and easier to store on Nur. Sadly, as you already know, the world I'm running doesn't have the best facilities to produce them locally."

"It would be quite fortunate to be able to treat 200 of my bodyguards to some nice donuts. Perhaps rewarding 500 members of my highest-performing staff with a pastry that reminds them of home would help me immensely. They deserve a good cake, wouldn't you agree?"

Even when asked about herself, her mind never seemed to stray far from the welfare of her workers. Although, her attempt to get you to do all the financing and delivery is as blatant as it is good-humored.
options: 1:UAFVNBackOption:"I'll... see what I can do. Thank you for your time, Lady Alexandra"

text:
"Why do you ask? Do you wish to excite me with sweets, like you did with the other officials?" 

The queen giggled with a warm smile while pointing her pen at the general direction of the Vermilion Station, referring to a certain someone. "Hehe, I know you've been using your charisma to gain the trust of my subjects." She drops her pen and taps on her desk, causing a  holodisplay to materialize. As the Permaisuri scrolls through, you gather that she's looking at a list of past and present staff members.

"Well... If you're that generous. I suppose you could donate some delightful pastries to my local administrative office here in New November. How about a bit of everything from that famous bakery shop, Interdimensional Confectionary? A couple hundred boxes would do nicely. Hmm... 400 cakes, 200 donuts and 200 pretzels should do nicely. I'm sure you could figure it out somehow." Aeria giggles wondering if you'll accept her simple challenge.
options: 1:UAFVNBackOption:"I will consult my quartermaster, your Majesty."

text:
"What a strange inquiry. This unit does not require any consumable or external power source to function." She pivots to observe you directly. From behind her opaque visor, it was impossible to discern whether she was merely addressing you, or analyzing your every move against some omniscient database.

"However, my fellow bio-colleagues in AED's Research Division at the Naval Headquarters surely would appreciate some delicious gifts from New Aurora. Approximately 800 boxes of pretzels and croissants are required. Do this and your standing with me will improve, slightly." AER-1A dragged one of her myriad holo-displays to her side. It showed information about the items in question and where on Aurora you could find them.

"Favorite snacks, you say?" 

She presses her finger on her lips, pondering. "Usually, I enjoy more refined things like Blue Lobster Thermidor or any imported luxury goods that traders normally bring into the station, but if you must ask what is my little part-time treat, it's chocolate."  

She chuckles as she place her hands down on her lap. "The divine taste of from my home planet's aromatic chocolate sets my heart adrift. From November City's famous mochas to the filled chocolates of Casavenca." She taps her finger on her chin and clears her throat, furling her brows and smirking with interest.

"If you're offering, I'd love to have 350 Pretzels & Croissant bundles to share with the members of my administrative offices."
"Favorite snack? No, I'm not interested in that."

text:
"You want that activated? hmm.."

The Administrator ponders for a moment. 

"Sure, I can activate it for you, however, I'll need a core device."
She pauses for a moment to send some material data to your holopad, it concerns some kind of rock.

"That thing is called acoustic resonator metal, I'll need you to fetch it from Nur Mining Planet, you know, Alexandra's. With the material needed, I can help authorizing the use of the deep space ping from eyeseau"

"Know that I don't usually do this for anybody, its a powerful feature of Eyeseau vessel that is turned off by default."
"Great, I'll be waiting for your return. Good luck out there."

"I see, so you've acquired the device, then let us begin the preparation."

"Please meet me in the docking bay."
The holo-screen turns off.

"Here at last." You can see the figure of The Administrator already waiting on the docking bay with some of her fully armed bodyguards.
"I do this because I trust that you won't misuse this ability." She stares at you and continues, she gets inside the eyeseau towards the command room and promptly activates the dormant function of Eyeseau. 

Not long after, a message appears on the screen.
"Omniscan Activated"

"Remember, with this, I placed a great amount of my trust in you, don't let me down."
She gets off the command room and left you with the new feature.

You have to remain vigilant as UAF might have more enemies than you expected should you accept this commission.

Also, you are free to resign, of course, as you're just a freelance contractor and not permanently attached to the federation. Personally I wouldn't recommend backing off from your word though."

id: # Missions, change text for mission todo

text:
"There are no available missions for you at the moment, try coming back later."
OR
You can see Nia is being distracted by something off-screen.
"Ah, yeah, there are none at the moment, I'll inform you if I have anything"

"Nya!"
"According to my files, there are no suitable mission that outsider like you might take right now." She said in stern voice.

"That's one mission available, you might want to take it." Nia said as the prompt appears on your screen.
OR
"Just in time for this mission, you are lucky." Nia drags over the mission details to you, allowing you to see the detail if you wish to.

"So, anything sounds good for you right now?" Prompts appear shortly after her explaination.
OR
"Pick one and I'll explain about them" Prompts appear shortly after her explaination.
OR
She quickly drags over the missions prompt to you after explaining as she is feeding Inna. "I'll be back to you in a sec, I'll have to do something, just pick one you are interested in"

"Alright, hopefully you can do it well, $playerName."
OR
"Don't disappoint me, alright?"
OR
"Well, that's one headache off of me" Nia smiles as you picked the missions.
OR
"I'll be back to these paperwork then, try not to mess up badly." Nia said as her gaze slowly back to her paperwork after you accepted the mission.

"Access denied, we are currently limiting contact with unknown spacefarer, for more information please visit "Aurora Diplomatic Bureau"."

Someone said from the other end of the speaker.

You begin to make your approach towards an opulent white building with an exterior silver lettered sign that reads "Stjarna's House of Nobles. Home to House LaFayette Genetics'." You show your official invitation paperwork to a group of UAF soldiers guarding the door who analyze your credentials thoroughly before they nod and allowed you passage while giving directions to access the Main director's office. 

Passing the main lobby and through a clean white hallway, what you do notice apart from the occasional noble representation is the number of scientists walking by you, some of them having animalistic features like the ears of a dog or tails like a cat, etc. Until finally, make it to the office of the current ruling noble of the station.

What sits behind that futuristic desk was a woman with hair that was lush, sunrise-gold hair, an elegant face that gives off a tender smile, a fit well-formed figure with a close-fitting uniform that appears to be custom-made compared to the standard uniforms you have seen so far, and a personality radiates with ladylike temperament. You get a good look at her features and what was more baffling was that this woman that sits before you has both large pointed ears and a very soft, fluffy, well-groomed tail that swings left and right casually like a fox; similar to her hair color. She suddenly clears her throat and sits straight to address you.

"Welcome $playerName, I have heard of a freelance captain rising in popularity within the UAF, My files describe you to be steadfast and courageous at your best when the situation demands it with a more loose connection with the other factions, truly a free spirit compared to the other ruling factions of the universe."

She smiles cordially as she nods with approval

"Nia was right about you being a captain of potential and respectable qualities, and I sure hope such talents could aid not only her majesty, but myself included."

She suddenly burst into a hearty laugh as she realized she forgot some important details to tell you. "OH! Forgive me, where are my manners.~ My name is Inari "Masoko" LaFayette, the current leading administrator and the main ruling house of the Stjarna Sector."

"If you have not been brought up to speed Captain, this is Hem Fayette Station. We are the main providers of fuel for the Federation, but this station also serves as a main military garrison for the current solar system. And to most of my staff and the people under my governance, it is our home away from home."

She steps aside and gestures towards the window and the view behind her. From where you are, you see a stunning panoramic view of the city within Hem Fayette from a clean modern suburb filled with lush and verdant plants that naturally grow on New Auroria to an extensive city center filled with faint lights and colorful holographic billboards of Aurorian advertisements.

Your observant eyes also noticed that they have agricultural farms hidden within bio-domes and a large mountain range in the distance as if they are trying to copy a part of New Aurorian's landscape in detail. The immersion was broken as soon as Inari tapped your shoulder and gives you a light-hearted smile before turning her attention to the city behind the window.

script:
$player.metUAFInari = true
FireAll PopulateOptions
AddPotentialContact
FireAll PopulateOptions
text:
"It took us years to elevate this station to the paradise we live in now and the queen has chosen my family to be the governors who guide these people like the northern star in the night sky. This responsibility now rests upon my shoulders and I shall be certain that my people's lives here are comfortable ones."

After sharing her perception on the matter, she turns to you and gives a small chuckle. "My apologies Captain.~ I will say, I do envy your freedom to choose for yourself out there, venturing among the stars." She quickly tidies up her uniform before sitting back down. "Now, let us discuss more pressing matters."

You wait for your turn to be called in when the secretary finally tells you that Inari may see you now. You walk down the same hallway and go through the automated doors to see her.
OR
You approach the front desk only for the secretary to hand you a communication tablet and tells you that Inari is open to talk. However, she is currently busy with interplanetary affairs. You sit down in one of the sofa's and turn the communicator on.

Inari looks at you professionally "Greetings to you captain $playerName, what is it you wish to ask from the nobles?"
OR
Inari is busy with some paperwork, she glances at you quickly and waves her hand swiftly before focusing on what she's writing on. "Kindly skip the pleasantries for now captain, interplanetary affairs can be such a chore."
OR
Inari looks back at you attentively, her ears perkes up to listen. "House Fayette may now hear your request captain."

You watch Inari quickly fix up her uniform as you enter. "Apologise captain, just doing my best to look my best." She said with a wink. "Now then, is there anything you wish to discuss with me?"
OR
Inari was on call with Nia, as soon as she noticed $playerName in her presence she decided to wrap things up "We can discuss the situation later Nia, I have other matters to attend too." she ends the call and places her hands together "The joys to be a freelance~ Anyways, do you need anything captain?"
OR
"Why hello Captain~" she smiles as she brushes her hair over her ears with her hand. "Have you considered joining my genetics program? You would make a great volunteer." She smiles as she cedes the floor to you and what you wish to discuss with her.

She turns to you with a joyful smile, her tail shoot up in excitement when she notices you. "Well, if it isn't my favorite captain.~ Is there something you need or perhaps something you want to tell me personally~." Her ears flicker and her tail wags back and forth with interest in what you have to say.
OR
"Captain $playerName,.~ It makes me happy you would come back to talk to me again.~" Her tail wags from side to side. "Shall we talk about your adventures over some wine and cheese?" 
OR
"From the distance stars, you return to me." she chuckles softly with a tender hearted smile "I would love to give you a hug captain. If only to grant you some comfort from the burdens space travel may bring. But I am afraid that is not within my in a position to do so." She says observantly "Now,~ How are you feeling?"

She lifts her hands and waves goodbye slowly "Goodbye Captain, may the glory of Her Majesty and her people give you the strength to carry on."

"Access denied, direct communication to the administrator of this regional station is restricted, for more information please visit Aurora Diplomatic Bureau."

Your request to speak to the administrator of this station has been rejected, you probably need to go to the bureau office they mentioned earlier and get some sort of permit.

"So, you've finally come to Nur Station, huh?" a raspy voice calls out to you. "The administrator in the diplomatic bureau recommended your services to me, no idea what you did to gain such recognition, but we will see what you can do."

You turn to find a woman approaching you, flanked by numerous guards with various cybernetics installed on their body. With you and your security team vastly outnumbered, you decide it's better to not mess with her or get on her bad side. "Don't be so tense, welcome to Nur Processing Plant, here we ensure that UAF always gets the resources it needs, especially rare ores and minerals. But before anything else, I would like to test your capabilities, $playerName." Despite her egalitarian speech, her face stays serious the whole time.

"Since you are here, I need someone who can deliver these materials to somewhere, are you up to the task?"
