text: You put down the TriPad and slide it over to the stern man first. Crow takes it and carefully examines the report, one page at a time. Mantis lowers her head, her eyes hidden by the visor of her cap. The TriPad changes hands. Aside from Mantis, everyone takes their eventual turn. They look at each other and nod before proceeding.

Crow's voice was slightly coarse on the ears. "As we suspected, this is yet another isolated but connected case related to the aforementioned threat. It may seem rather benign at first. Ordinary vessels crewed by human flesh and blood, but they are not. You may also recognize the men and women who served on those ships but they are not who you think they are. We have an official designation for this deceitful enemy. We call them 'Mimics'."  No one is shocked at the calmly delivered revelation.

"From what we currently have, thanks to the combined efforts of Persephone’s STRACOM and Empress’s AINTCOM along with other notable contributors, we should be able to detect patterns that could aid us in the future. Towards that end, we are hard at work acquiring more data and resources. One thing is for certain, we are no longer safe out here at the fringe of the Persean Sector."

script:
BeginConversation uaf_admiral_naggy_na true true
ShowSecondPerson uaf_commodore_lyora
ShowThirdPerson uaf_rear_admiral_davos_tamor
text:
Vice Admiral Naggy raises a question. "Could these entities be some variant of the Remnants? Or the new ‘threats’ that come from the Abyssal Shore beyond the Caeli System?" A holographic display lights up, showing the mapped regions around Auroran space. He points his finger towards the western side of Caeli.

"No… it can’t be." Lyora shakes her head, recalling the fight. "They may have been ghostly but they fought like we did. UAF doctrine at its basic."

"The commodore is correct, they are something different. An entity that can accurately replicate technology and organisms indistinguishable from the originals has found its way here." Crow's voice is cold and callous.

Mantis raises her head, revealing a cold stare. Her dark blue eyes are placid, gazing toward an unknown horizon.

An uneasy silence keeps the atmosphere cold. Vice Admiral Naggy was the first to break the silence. "Well, it would seem we have our work cut out for us, ladies and gentlemen. I must be getting back. Commodore, shall I give you a lift back to your quarters?"

Lyora nodded slowly. "Yeah... I need more hours in the simulator." The commodore was shaken, but she definitely had the fire of retribution in her eyes. The two made their exit. With the briefing concluded, you stood to follow them out.

"Starfarer, a word." Crow's words are polite but his cold tone leaves no room for refusal.

text:
"As you can see, the Federation’s survival may depend on how much we can learn about these extra-terrestrial threats. However, we've deemed it appropriate to enact an information blackout on the topic, avoiding panic among our ranks and the public is vital at this time. Thus, you will report to me directly for further assignments and you will be compensated for your efforts."

Crow whispers something in Mantis' ear. She gives a nod in response. Crow gestures his hand to the door, giving you permission to leave. "That'll be all, Starfarer."

Do feel free to submit your feedback to this address in Nova Drive Yards. She'll... see what she can do." You hear a chime from your personal terminal. The address at Nova Drive Yards leads to a building off the main thoroughfare. Perhaps you'll remember to pass by one day.

text:
Remembering herself, she clears her throat. "Anyway, just wanted to say thanks for pulling me out of the fire. Since talk is cheap, I left something special for you back with your crew. Take care out there. I'll be seeing you." Lyora gives you a warm handshake before letting you be on your way. "Maybe I'll break some arrows on the way back."

You raise an eyebrow at the commodore's back, wondering if you heard her correctly. Probably one of those Auroran idioms.

"Greetings, captain. Stay focused and locked-in. Don't stray too far away from my fleet. We don't know what to expect out there so keeping each other within reasonable detection is vital."

"Captain, I'm readying weapons. I don't know who or what those are but if they're looking for a fight, I've got my guns loaded."

Lyora's exhausted face appears on your comm-screen. "Ah, captain…" Her mouth opens and closes finding the right words...

text: After landing, you run into Crow and Mantis. The man greets you with a soft acknowledgement before speaking,  "It seemed apt to mention that your entry and egress times via Caeli's Gate have been logged. As far as we are aware, no other group or individual is capable of such mode of transportation. Not even the Big Three. We shall remember this... unexpected development." Saying what he needed to say, he gives you a nod before leaving, linking up with Mantis at the turn of the walkway, disappearing from view.
options: UAFLingeringEchoesDavosExtraSceneGate_Leave:Be on your way to the docks

text: As you walk through the hangar, you notice the hangar crews behaving strangely. They speak in hushed tones and frantic whispers, eyes filled with raw emotions. Disbelief. Nervousness. You see dozens of them crowded at a viewing port, their whispers sounding more frenzied.

Those that notice you give you a wide berth. As you walk through the crowd, curious to what has grabbed their attention, you see it. The Ziggurat. A striking ship under your command. Seeing an alien ship so close to their station must have frightened them.

You're not sure when Crow and Mantis started to stand beside you, but by the time you notice, you make eye contact with the man. Crow's gaze narrows... You feel he has more to say, but decides to hold off... for now.

Security personnel arrive to ease the commotion, encouraging everyone to resume their respective posts. Crow and Mantis depart without a word, gone as silently as they came. Within the hour, UAF marines have cordoned off the Ziggurat and have also increased the security presence for your docked ships. The deployment seems to ease some of the fears of the station's inhabitants. Only time will tell if this will hold true.
options: UAFLingeringEchoesDavosExtraSceneZiggy_Leave:Be on your way to the docks

text: As you exit your ship, you are greeted by a platoon of marines instead of the usual security duo. After briefly interacting with a not-so-kind platoon sergeant to verify the purpose of your visit, you are informed about your marine escort for the duration of your stay. The squad of marines' escorting you give you strange looks. They aren't the only ones. The UAF personnel that pass you by all give you angry glares, or looks of dread.

text: The marines refuse to answer you and continue to escort you to the lobby where Crow and Mantis are waiting. Mantis is unreadable, only slightly squinting her eyes at you. Crow's eyes only give you... contempt? Disgust? Or is it hatred? The atmosphere in the hangar feels heavy, almost suffocating. The crews yell foreign words at you. Again, you are about to ask what this is about when Crow gestures for them to stop with a gentle show of his hand. His words are as coarse as they are brief. "Follow me."

He gestures to the marines, and they seem content to leave you in Crow's company. The pair take you to a secluded lounge, out of sight. About an hour passes by in silence. Mantis checks her holopad, nodding at Crow. "Seems like we're done here." Crow seems to have returned to his usual callousness. "Do let me know if something like this happens again." He and Mantis stand to leave.

A new holoscreen appears as he taps his pen on the table gently. His face is stoic as he continues. "Thanks to STRACOM, AINTCOM and other relevant offices, we managed to compile enough information to believe that your target might be lingering somewhere in $bcb_systemName, $bcb_dist light-years from here."

"Due to the sensitive nature of this mission, an information blackout is in effect but it won’t last long until someone starts pulling at the good yarn we've spun. We estimated that you might have up to $bcb_days days to complete your task."

"I am officially authorized by the Integrated General Headquarters over at New Aurora to compensate you for your efforts. We will grant you $bcb_reward credits plus an additional amount in local currency upon mission completion, including a bonus for maintaining your silence over this matter." The screen fades away as the briefing ends. "So, are you up to the task, Starfarer?"

"We have a situation. Some kind of entity is engaging surveyors on the fringes of the sector. Damages are confirmed, so it needs to be dealt with immediately."

The usually tired man seems more haggard than usual. Crow continues, "However, we have no active or pending missions in that area. Empress’ forces are already stretched thin and she is unable to spare any recon elements beyond our controlled space at this time, so we need you to investigate with utmost caution."

"Whatever they're up to, High Command doesn't want the hostile contact to endanger anyone anymore. Furthermore, we have enough evidence to support that it could be the Mimics due to the force's unique quirks based on their behavior and movements."

"The Automata Intelligence Command has detected the first sighting of an unknown contact in the following sectors, away from known civilizations."

Crow shows a holovid of blurry shapes and imaging-ghosts. "They managed to lose the recon elements trailing them. As AINTCOM's assets are currently needed elsewhere, we’re unable to maintain surveillance and confirm its nature. Your job is to pick up the trail and make sure this is nothing new."

"Discover the nature of this anomaly and act accordingly to the situation. If they turn out to be what I initially suspected, feel free to defend yourself with extreme prejudice."

"Vice Admiral Persephone from Strategic Command has reported the presence of a wandering fleet that bears Auroran signatures and failed to respond to their repeated hails."

You nod, suspecting it might be related to the Mimics. Crow continues, "We were lucky to detect this one early before it could develop into something more dangerous. So we need you to investigate before it could potentially affect anyone that unknowingly comes within its vicinity."

"Verify their identity. If they are Mimics, have your men analyze them and record any data that might be useful."

script: AddText"With all the people milling about the plaza, you’d be forgiven for thinking that the United Aurora Federation had lived here for hundreds of years. Fashion stores, cafés, and restaurants broken up by the occasional military surplus store and military checkpoint. Most of the bureaucracy was hidden away within the skyscrapers that make up the city’s administrative center. For any other resident of the Persean Sector, it's eerily peaceful here, far removed from the violence and cut throats of the core worlds. Had it not been for all the stores being manned by Neukos and a vast majority of the pedestrians being in uniform, you would have never guessed that this was a military HQ and not a civilian resort world." textGrayColor

AddText"After making your way through the underground hangar complex, you come across an open-air concourse… Well, open-space concourse. The shield over the crater’s surface protects the area from a variety of hazards, whether that be meteorites, or falling debris and stray attacks from space battles. A variety of vendors are scattered around, booths showing off various goods and holos of ships for sale. Above are the silhouettes of the Lunamun shipyards, backlit by a crystal clear view of New Aurora. A breathtaking sight compared to when looking at Lunamun from New Aurora." textGrayColor

AddText"Aboard Vermillion Station, you are greeted by an all too familiar sight: Thousands of spacers waiting around for permits, trades, and authorization. Aside from the handful of clean booths maintained and cleaned by Aurorans, most are occupied by merchants from other factions, though a majority are independent, selling possessions for the credits to establish a new life here. The handful of merchants from the core worlds stick out like a sore thumb and are all boothed close together, their security teams working hand in hand for their collective security. With the disgruntled look on the refugees' faces, it makes sense that they all band together to protect themselves from bitter, out-of-luck spacers." textGrayColor

AddText"Looking over the concourse, the pirates and miners of Entiaris mingle here freely. Despite the difference in architecture, the concourse looks virtually indistinguishable from any other core world pirate hub, save for the tattered Federation banners have been ripped apart and used as rigging, ropes, and other various tethers to tie down the myriad spoils brought in by the pirates. You can see the faint glow of the old shipyards in the sky, lit up by the numerous welding torches tearing apart stolen vessels and disassembling Federation hardware for valuable components." textGrayColor

AddText"While controlled by the UAF, there are relatively few uniformed personnel amidst the masses. Neukos are virtually non-existent, standing only behind counters with armed Federation guards. Assorted parts and forcefully disconnected limbs suggest that there used to be more. However, given that there is now a pirate gang carrying out drug deals behind the bar, it's safe to say they were removed in order to prevent theft and sales of “cybernetics” on the black market. The mercenaries that replaced them are notably less friendly, and far less professional. And you’re fairly certain at least some of them are just pirates playing dress up." textGrayColor

AddText"For a shipyard, the observation-deck-turned-main-concourse of the Nova Drive Yards played host to a wide variety of goods and services as well as a dedicated dealership for new ships. The massive viewports gave an almost uninterrupted view of the surrounding shipyards, where a great many ships moved in and out for maintenance, some filled with brand new ships under construction. However, the biggest attraction was the dock nearest to the concourse: from here, prospective buyers could select a ship to purchase, and watch it be built in person." textGrayColor

AddText"The open-air plaza was bustling with tourists going booth to booth. Ice cream stands, souvenir shops, and tourist attractions were everywhere. Most notable were the Neuko hives, which in addition to maintaining all the individual units, allowed for the renting out of civilian models for various purposes, including tour guides, baby sitters, and companionship among other things. You feel a little out of place, still wearing your space suit, but you’re here on business right now, you can explore the attractions another time." textGrayColor

AddText"Compared to the regular branches however, interests in anything related to the Aurorans are exceptionally high, with Tri-Tachyon officials constantly hound traders with questions about the Aurorans and blatantly seeking out any Auroran technologies or information. Some lucrative contracts offered by Tri-Tachyon are notably suspicious as well, revolving around 'secret missions' and 'corporate espionage'." textGrayColor

AddText"Makeshift stalls and improvised shelves are in abundance on Los Scrapyard. Every eye here is keen on profit, trying to peddle something from the scrap heap, searching for a bargain that will help keep their mouths fed and their bodies warm. People here are shifty at best and itching to pull the trigger at worst. Los Scrappers will generally stay out of minor disputes so long as trade isn't too badly disturbed. However, get on their wrong side and they'll give you a one-way direct trip to Atropos. Via airlock." textGrayColor

AddText"Lanun has clearly seen better days. Through the dim, antiquated lighting (sometimes even candlelight), one can see where one occupant built upon their predecessor's work. Vast work areas for mineral processing suddenly give way to trade squares and bazaars, only to be watched by Hegemony bunker slits overseeing fields of fire. The people of Lanun are fiery, especially in such proximity to the Federation. Many have heard of the soulless monsters wrought from the false paradise of New Aurora. Many more are all too eager to take up arms against the foreign infidels. Young fighters are born on Lanun every day, and many await the day of reckoning that will arrive at the Aurorans' threshold." textGrayColor
